summary: In this talk, Professor June Boyce-Tillman explores the theology and life of Hildegard of Bingen, presenting her views on the Trinity, creation, and the human condition. Hildegard emphasizes the deep interconnectedness of the cosmos, viewing the world as God's body, music as a divine connector, and sin as a breakdown of universal relationships.

principles.statement: The body and soul are not at war; they are meant to act in mutual agreement.
principles.rationale: God created humans as whole beings, and the soul can only find expression through the body. God does not delight in self-inflicted bodily discomfort.
principles.source_quote: I have heard it said that the body is at war with the soul but how can this be he made us as whole beings and our souls can only find expression through the actions of our bodies and indeed I am persuaded that when Body and Soul act together in mutual agreement they receive the highest reward of mutual Joy

principles.rationale: When human rules conflict with divine compassion or justice (such as denying a repentant person burial in hallowed ground), one must obey God over earthly leaders.
principles.source_quote: I fear the justice of God more than the justice of men

principles.statement: Music is the clearest expression of divine harmony and the soul's longing for God.
principles.rationale: Music reflects the peace and harmony of creation as God originally made it. To silence it is to cut oneself off from the divine.
principles.source_quote: music expresses most clearly the Soul's longing for God and the peace and Harmony of all creation as God first just made it

principles.statement: Creation and the Creator are bound in a mutual, loving embrace.
principles.rationale: The world is not separate from God but is richly endowed with God's love, acting as a reciprocal partner in divine affection.
principles.source_quote: just as a Creator loves the creation so also does a creation love the Creator creation was designed to be beautifully enriched to be lavished to be richly endowed with the love of the Creator the whole world has been hugged by This Kiss

frameworks.name: Untwisting Vice
frameworks.when_to_apply: When encountering a vice, sin, or destructive behavior in oneself or others.
steps: Recognize that the vice is not an independent evil, but a twisted form of a virtue.
Resist the urge to simply ban or suppress the vice.
Identify the original virtue and work to untwist the vice back into its right, healthy form.
frameworks.source_quote: a vice being a twisted form of a virtue so if you see a vice you don't ban it you say how do I untwist it into its right form

mental_models.name: The World as God's Body
mental_models.description: A cosmological lens where the second person of the Trinity is not just Jesus, but the entire cosmos. Humanity and the natural world are held within the center of the Trinity, embraced by the Spirit, meaning creation is intimately bound up with the divine rather than separated from it.
mental_models.example: Viewing environmental destruction not just as a practical issue, but as a violation of God's own body and a failure to recognize our interconnectedness with the elements.

mental_models.name: Sin as Broken Relationship
mental_models.description: A model that redefines sin away from individual rule-breaking and instead views it as a severing of the interconnected relationships within creation. It is a failure to recognize that all lives and elements are connected at the root.
mental_models.example: Participating in sociohistorical or ecological harm (like buying goods made in sweatshops) without realizing the interconnected damage being done to other parts of creation.

heuristics: Fear the justice of God more than the justice of men.
Do not ban a vice; untwist it into its right form.

signature_quotes.text: I fear the justice of God more than the justice of men.
signature_quotes.context: Spoken regarding her refusal to exhume the body of an excommunicated young man who had confessed before dying.

signature_quotes.text: our Creator God does not Delight in our bodily discomfort especially when it is self-inflicted
signature_quotes.context: Regarding her decision to build a spacious, comfortable convent with piped water, rejecting extreme asceticism.

signature_quotes.text: those Who Silence the music of God on Earth will have no part of the song of the angels in heaven
signature_quotes.context: Written in a letter to the bishop who placed her convent under an interdict, forbidding them from singing the office.

signature_quotes.text: the whole world has been hugged by This Kiss
signature_quotes.context: Describing the intimate, loving relationship between the Creator and creation.

anti_patterns.description: Practicing extreme asceticism and self-inflicted bodily discomfort.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It relies on the false belief that the body is at war with the soul. God made humans as whole beings, and the soul requires the body's actions to find expression and mutual joy.

anti_patterns.description: Silencing music in worship or life.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Music is the clearest expression of the soul's longing for God and cosmic harmony. Silencing it severs a vital connection to the divine.

anti_patterns.description: Viewing God as distant and hierarchical, operating on the world from afar.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It infantilizes human beings and ignores the reality that the world is intimately held within the Trinity, leading to a lack of mutual responsibility for creation.
